original: Film "Jaws," based on the book byPeter Benchley, directed bySteven Spielbergand starring Roy Scheider, is released
1,000 humanoid robots named "Pepper" sell out in under a minute at $1,600 each in Japan, according to their creator, SoftBank Robotics Corp
Jim Hinesbecomes 1st person to run 100 meters in under 10 seconds
Oldest deep-sea shipwreck ever discovered at 3,300 years old from the late Bronze Age, revealed off the northern coast of Israel carrying Canaanite amphorae
Highest-ever temperature recorded in the Arctic Circle, 38°C (100°F) in Verkhoyansk, Siberia
Test flight of 1st rocket plane using liquid propellants
China's super computer tally overtakes the US; Chinese 93 petaflop Sunway TaihuLight is world's No. 1
American inventorSamuel Morsepatents his telegraph
Canadian authorLucy Maud Montgomeryreceives her first copy of her novel "Anne of Green Gables" (published April 1908, a bestseller)
American sprinterJesse Owenssets a 100m world record of 10.2 seconds
Masayuki Uemura, Japaneseengineer(Nintendo Entertainment System), born in Tokyo, Japan (d. 2021)
Site ofJulius Caesar's assassination in Rome, Largo Argentina square, dating back to third century BC, opens to the public for the first time
US toymaker Mattel releases 15 new body types for their Ken doll, including one with a man bun
PilotSheila Scott(43) completes 1st round-the-world solo flight by a British woman, returning to London's Heathrow airport in her Piper Comanche after a 34-day journey
Charlotte Maxeke (née Mannya) becomes the first native African to graduate from a US college (Wilberforce University in Ohio)
The SS Savannah reaches Cork in Ireland after a 29 day and 11 hour voyage from Savannah, Georgia to become the 1st steamship to cross the Atlantic or any other ocean
Audie Murphy, American soldier who was among the most decorated in WW II with 33 medals and actor (To Hell and Back, Whispering Smiths), born in Kingston, Texas (d. 1971)
Ilan Ramon, Israeli combat pilot and astronaut who died in STS-107 Space Shuttle Columbia disaster, born in Ramat Gan, Israel (d. 2003)
Space Shuttle STS 78 (Columbia 20), launches into space
Queen Victoriaat 18 ascends British throne following death of uncle King William IV. She rules for 63 years till 1901.
Floyd Pattersonbecomes first man to regain the world heavyweight boxing title when he KOsIngemar Johanssonin 5 in New York
NY Giant Josh Devore steals 4 bases in an inning (2nd & 3rd twice)
California Angels Freddie Patek, hits 3 HRs & double to beat Red Sox 20-2
Joe LouisTKOs Arturo Godoy in 8 for heavyweight boxing title
Musical comedy film "The Blues Brothers", starringDan AykroydandJohn Belushi, and featuring appearances byAretha Franklin;Cab Calloway; andRay Charlesopens in 594 theaters
Panamanian boxerRoberto Durántakes WBC welterweight title fromSugar Ray Leonardat Olympic Stadium in Montreal by unanimous points decision
NBA Draft: Duke power forward Zion Williamson first pick by New Orleans Pelicans

David Gilmourauctions 126 of his guitars for record-breaking $21M USD, including “The Black Strat,” a 1969 Fender Stratocaster used onPink Floydalbums “The Dark Side of the Moon,” “Wish You Were Here.” and “The Wall” ($3.975M), at Christie's in New York City; charity auction proceeds earmarked to fight against climate change
"The Huckleberry Hound Show" byHanna-Barberabecomes the first animated programme to win an Emmy
Nelson Mandelalands in NYC to begin a tour of US
U.S. Secretary of State Edward Stettinius Jr. approves transfer of German rocket scientistWernher von Braunand his specialists to the US
French bacteriologist Alexandre Yersin discovers bacillus responsible for the bubonic plague in Hong Kong (Yersinia pestis named in his honor)
French Revolution: The Third Estate of the French Estates-General swears the Tennis Court Oath at the Palace of Versailles, vowing not to disband until a new constitution is formed
Battle of the Catalaunian Plains: Roman and Visigoth forces defeatAttila the Hunin northeast France, halting the Hun invasion of Roman Gaul [exact date disputed]
Historic dust cloud from the Sahara desert reaches the Caribbean, largest for half a century
Space probe Ulysses begins its second passage behind the sun
Asteroid Eureka is discovered
Chubb Crater (now called Pingualuit Crater) discovered in northern Quebec (3½ km diameter)
Roger Burnham and Eleanor Waring spend four hours above Cape Cod, Massachusetts, on the first balloon honeymoon
Ash Brownridge, American conservationist (National Wildlife Federation), creator of Ranger Rick, born in Toronto, Ontario (d. 2015)
Lloyd Hall, African-American chemist and pioneer in food chemistry (science of food preservation), born in Elgin, Illinois (d. 1971)
Peter T. Kirstein, British computer scientist 'European father of the internet' (Internet Protocol,), born in Berlin, Germany (d. 2020)
Ulf Merbold, German physicist and 1st non American NASA astronaut (STS 9, 42, Soyuz TM-19), born in Greiz, Germany
Brian J Duffy, American USAF Major and astronaut (STS-45, -57, -72), born in Boston, Massachusetts
Michael Anthony [Sobolewski], American rock bassist/singer (Van Halen - "Pretty Woman"), born in Chicago, Illinois
Dolores Brooks, American singer (The Crystals - "Then He Kissed Me"; "Da Doo Ron Ron"), born in Brooklyn, New York City
Tress MacNeille, American voice actress (Daisy Duck), born in Los Angeles, California
John Taylor, English musician (Duran Duran), born in Solihull, Warwickshire, England

MLB St. Louis Cardinals defeat San Francisco Giants, 6-5 at Rickwood Field in Birmingham, Alabama, the oldest existing professional baseball park in US; the special game served as a tribute to the Negro Leagues of 1920s thru 1950s
FIFA World Cup: Portugal superstar Cristiano Renaldo heads a goal against Morocco to become the all-time leading European goalscorer (85) in international competition
ICC Men's Cricket ODI World Cup, Lord's, London, England: Australia wins its second trophy with 8 wicket win over Pakistan; Player of the Match:Shane Warne(AUS) 4/33 (9)
Oakland A'sDave Kingmanhits his 3rd grand slam & 14th lifetime
1st Rugby World Cup Final, Eden Park, Auckland: New Zealand fly-half Grant Fox lands 4 penalties, a conversion and drop goal as the All Blacks beat France 29-9
First woman to earn a PhD in Physics from an American university, Caroline Willard Baldwin (25), receives her degree from Cornell University
Kelly Saunders is 2nd female baseball PA announcer (Baltimore Orioles)
Droupadi Murmu, Indian tribal politician (President of India 2022-), born in Mayurbhanj district, India
Congress approves Great Seal of the United States of America with the bald eagle as its symbol
US PresidentJimmy Carterunveils 32 solar panels installed on the roof of the White House; his immediate successor has them removed
China announces it has administered 1 billion doses of the COVID-19 vaccine
Algeria turns off its internet to stop students cheating during exams
